reachability_fact:
Every mask site in both files is either an identity cast or is protected by a real guard before it:

babel.iii:45 — `(s + ((i as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64))` in babel_buf_str: i is u32 so the mask is identity; real bound is `i < n` (line 44). babel_buf_str has no @export and no external caller in the entire STDLIB tree (grep of `from "babel.iii"` returns only the six @export fns: reset/used/byte_at/version/check_version/compute_envelope_mhash).

babel.iii:66 — same argument for babel_buf_hex_bytes (also module-private, no external caller).

babel_intent.iii:52 — `(src + ((i as u64) & 0xFFFFu64))` in babel_int_emit_str: mask 0xFFFF=65535 is identity for `i < len` where len is always one of {47,14,13,23,18} (the five fixed-size literal strings). Module-private fn.

babel_intent.iii:110 — `intent_required_guarantees(...) as u64 & 0xFFFFFFFFu64`: this is a VALUE mask on a u32 widened to u64, not a buffer index at all.

babel_intent.iii:123 — `(dst_buf + ((k as u64) & 0xFFFu64))` in the copy loop: the precheck at line 120 (`if (off as u32) > dst_cap { return 0u32 }`) refuses before the loop runs if the envelope is oversized; and the fixed-structure envelope is structurally bounded at ~159 bytes (47+16+14+1+1+13+1+16+1+23+1+18+1+1 = fixed literals only, confirmed by counting the babel_int_emit_str/hex16/dec calls in babel_intent_send). The mask 0xFFF=4095 is identity on the reachable range ~0..159.

babel_intent.iii:136-137 — `_babel_eq_at` pointer arithmetic: an overflow-safe subtraction guard at line 133 (`off > srclen - patlen`) fires before any access; the `& 0xFFFF` casts on both off and i are identity since the guard already proves off+patlen <= srclen <= 2^32-1 and i < patlen <= srclen.

babel_intent.iii:170 — `(src_len as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64`: identity on a u32, not an index.

External caller audit (grep `from "babel.iii"` and `from "babel_intent.iii"` across all .iii sources): babel.iii only exports reset/used/byte_at/version/check_version/compute_envelope_mhash — none of the internal buffer-writer functions. babel_intent.iii is called only from corpus/903_babel_intent.iii with buf=BIBUF[4096] and dst_cap=4096, which the dst_cap precheck at line 120 already gates correctly.
maximal_design:
Because all mask sites are provably cosmetic-identity, the no-compromise maximal improvement is NOT to add redundant explicit-size guards (which would be refactor theatre). Instead, the subsystem is made strictly better by two structural improvements:

1. Drop all the cosmetic `& 0xFFFFFFFFu64` / `& 0xFFFFu64` identity masks in pointer arithmetic — they add noise that obscures whether a real guard exists, and they fail the clarity test of the III NIH style. Replace them with clean direct casts: `(s + (i as u64))`, `(src + (off as u64) + (i as u64))`, etc. The compiler already zero-extends u32->u64 on `i as u64`; the mask is redundant.

2. Thread a capacity return through babel_int_emit_str so callers can detect overflow rather than silently discarding the -1 from babel_int_emit. Currently babel_int_emit_str always returns `len` even when some emits failed. This is the structural gap: the buf-overflow path is guarded by babel_int_emit(off >= 4096) but the failure is silently absorbed (off keeps incrementing). The maximal form is: babel_int_emit_str and babel_int_emit_hex16 and babel_int_emit_dec all return i32 (0=ok, -1=overflow), and babel_intent_send checks each return and bails immediately with 0u32 on overflow. This makes the overflow contract explicit and testable (the current code can only be tested by driving off >= 4096, which the fixed structure never reaches — but callers cannot rely on that structural argument being preserved across future edits).

No ABI change to exported functions: babel_intent_send/receive signatures stay identical. The change is purely internal to the module.

code_changes.anchor:
let sp : *u8 = (src + ((off as u64) & 0xFFFFu64) + ((i as u64) & 0xFFFFu64)) as *u8
        let pp : *u8 = (pat + ((i as u64) & 0xFFFFu64)) as *u8
code_changes.new_code:
let sp : *u8 = (src + (off as u64) + (i as u64)) as *u8
        let pp : *u8 = (pat + (i as u64)) as *u8
let last_off : u64 = src_len as u64
